title: QA: BlastRadiusGuard u32 saturation hides max_files_per_turn breach (guardrails.rs)
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
complexity
## Location
src/guardrails.rs:319
## Violation
The per-turn file counter compares a saturated `u32` against the limit:
```rust
files.insert(normalized);
if u32::try_from(files.len()).unwrap_or(u32::MAX) > self.config.max_files_per_turn {
    let msg = format!(
        "Blast radius: exceeded max files per turn ({}/{})",
        files.len(),
        self.config.max_files_per_turn
    );
```
If the operator configures `max_files_per_turn = u32::MAX` to "disable"
the limit, this comparison can never trigger (the saturated value is not
strictly greater than itself). If a session accumulates >u32::MAX file
entries (unlikely but possible on a long-running daemon), the counter
silently caps and the breach is invisible. There is no overflow signal
or log.
## Mandated refactor
Switch to `usize` end-to-end and compare against
`self.config.max_files_per_turn as usize` (after explicit overflow
check at config-load). Reserve `0` as "disabled" (already handled at
line 312) and document `u32::MAX` is not special. Add a `debug!` log
when files.len() crosses 50% of the limit so operators see drift before
hitting the cap.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
